title: Silent Springs, Windswept Seas: On the Environmental Vision of Rachel Carson
description: In her acceptance speech upon receiving the National Book Award for The Sea Around Us (1951), biologist and author Rachel Carson (1907–1964) explained: “If there is poetry in my book about the sea, it is not because I deliberately put it there,
hook: Rachel Carson wrote poetry about the sea without calling it poetry—how a scientist taught us to feel the living world through sentences.

title: Define Auteur: In defence of Under the Cherry Moon
description: The story of how Prince fought tooth and nail to retain full control over his directorial debut, which, 40 years later, demands new attention.
hook: Prince made a terrible movie in 1986 and fought everyone to do it his way. Forty years later, it's suddenly worth defending.

title: A Child’s World: Sloane Crosley on the Short Fiction of Katherine Mansfield
description: Oh, to live in a room filled with the objects described by Katherine Mansfield. To arrange her combs, playing cards, and enamel boxes, to try on “the most amusing orange coat with a procession of black monkeys round the hem.”
hook: Katherine Mansfield's stories are miniature rooms you can actually inhabit—her objects, her light, her bruised attention to living.
